Property.title: Property
Property.description: Represents a single rental property managed within the application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Property entity.

address.description: The full physical address of the rental property.

propertyType.description: The type of property (e.g., flat, house, bungalow, maisonette, studio, HMO).

status.description: The current occupancy status of the property (e.g., Vacant, Occupied, Under Maintenance).

bedrooms.description: The number of bedrooms in the property.

bathrooms.description: The number of bathrooms in the property.

notes.description: General notes or additional details about the property.

Tenant.title: Tenant
Tenant.description: Represents an individual tenant associated with a tenancy agreement.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Tenant entity.

name.description: The full name of the tenant.

email.description: The email address of the tenant.

phoneNumber.description: The primary telephone number of the tenant.

notes.description: Any specific notes or details regarding the tenant.

Tenancy.title: Tenancy
Tenancy.description: Details about a specific tenancy agreement linking tenants to a property for a defined period.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Tenancy entity.

tenantIds.description: References to Tenants included in this tenancy agreement. (Relationship: Tenancy N:N Tenant)

startDate.description: The official start date of the tenancy agreement.

endDate.description: The official end date of the tenancy agreement. Null if ongoing.

notes.description: Specific terms, conditions, or additional notes for this tenancy agreement.

MaintenanceRequest.title: MaintenanceRequest
MaintenanceRequest.description: Records details of a maintenance issue reported for a property.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MaintenanceRequest entity.

title.description: A brief summary or title for the maintenance request.

description.description: A detailed description of the maintenance issue.

urgency.description: The urgency level of the maintenance request (e.g., Low, Medium, High, Emergency).

status.description: The current status of the maintenance request (e.g., Open, In Progress, Resolved, Cancelled, Pending Quote).

reportedAt.description: The date and time when the maintenance request was reported.

resolvedAt.description: The date and time when the maintenance request was resolved, if applicable.

notes.description: Internal notes or remarks related to the maintenance request.

documentIds.description: References to documents or images related to this maintenance request. (Relationship: MaintenanceRequest N:N Document)

Inspection.title: Inspection
Inspection.description: Details about a scheduled or completed property inspection.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Inspection entity.

scheduledAt.description: The date and time when the inspection is scheduled.

type.description: The type of inspection (e.g., Move-in, Mid-term, Move-out, Annual, Other).

notes.description: Notes and observations from the inspection.

status.description: The current status of the inspection (e.g., Scheduled, Completed, Cancelled).

completedAt.description: The date and time when the inspection was completed, if applicable.

reportDocumentId.description: Reference to the primary inspection report document. (Relationship: Inspection 1:1 Document)

Document.title: Document
Document.description: Represents an uploaded file or image related to a property or other entities.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Document entity.

name.description: A descriptive name or title for the document (e.g., 'Front View Image', 'Tenancy Agreement').

fileUrl.description: The URL where the document file is stored.

documentType.description: The category or type of the document (e.g., Image, TenancyAgreement, PropertyCertificate, Other).

uploadedAt.description: The date and time when the document was uploaded.

description.description: An optional detailed description of the document's content or purpose.

ActivityLog.title: ActivityLog
ActivityLog.description: Logs various actions and events occurring within the application, primarily related to properties.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ActivityLog entity.

propertyId.description: Reference to Property, indicating which property the activity relates to. (Relationship: Property 1:N ActivityLog)

entityType.description: The type of the primary entity involved in the activity (e.g., Property, MaintenanceRequest, Inspection, Tenancy, Document).

entityId.description: The ID of the primary entity involved in the activity.

action.description: The specific action that occurred (e.g., Created, Updated, StatusChanged, DocumentUploaded, Scheduled, Resolved).

description.description: A human-readable summary of the activity.

timestamp.description: The date and time when the activity occurred.

definition.description: Stores user-specific profile data and metadata. While a 'User' entity was not explicitly provided, it's a fundamental part of user authentication. This document implicitly defines the 'ownerId' for all subcollections.
Contains details for a rental property. Each property document includes a denormalized 'ownerId' field (equal to userId in the path) for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the property.

params.name: propertyId
params.description: The unique identifier for the specific property.

definition.description: Stores details for a tenant. Each tenant document includes a denormalized 'ownerId' field (equal to userId in the path) for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this tenant record.

params.name: tenantId
params.description: The unique identifier for the specific tenant.

definition.description: Records details of a tenancy agreement. Each tenancy document includes a denormalized 'ownerId' field (equal to userId in the path) for authorization independence and references a property and tenants.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this tenancy record.

params.name: tenancyId
params.description: The unique identifier for the specific tenancy agreement.

definition.description: Logs maintenance issues related to a specific property. Each request includes denormalized 'ownerId' and 'propertyId' fields for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the property and the request.

params.name: propertyId
params.description: The unique identifier of the property to which the request belongs.

params.name: maintenanceRequestId
params.description: The unique identifier for the specific maintenance request.

definition.description: Details about property inspections. Each inspection includes denormalized 'ownerId' and 'propertyId' fields for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the property and the inspection.

params.name: propertyId
params.description: The unique identifier of the property to which the inspection belongs.

params.name: inspectionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the specific inspection.

definition.description: Stores references to uploaded files and images related to a specific property. Each document includes denormalized 'ownerId' and 'propertyId' fields for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the property and the document.

params.name: propertyId
params.description: The unique identifier of the property to which the document belongs.

params.name: documentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the specific document.

definition.description: Logs various actions and events for a specific property. Each log entry includes denormalized 'ownerId' and 'propertyId' fields for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the property and the activity log.

params.name: propertyId
params.description: The unique identifier of the property to which the activity log entry belongs.

params.name: activityLogId
params.description: The unique identifier for the specific activity log entry.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed following a strong principle of user-centric data ownership and authorization independence. All user-specific data is deeply nested under a `/users/{userId}` path, which serves as the primary authorization boundary. This design ensures that all data belongs to the authenticated user (`request.auth.uid`), simplifying security rules significantly. Each top-level entity owned by a user (Property, Tenant, Tenancy) resides in its own subcollection directly under the user's document, e.g., `/users/{userId}/properties/{propertyId}`. This makes ownership explicit in the path itself, eliminating the need for complex `get()` calls in security rules to determine who owns the parent.Authorization Independence is primarily achieved through this hierarchical path structure and by denormalizing the `ownerId` (which is `request.auth.uid`) into every document. For subcollections like Maintenance Requests, Inspections, Documents, and Activity Logs, they are nested under their respective `Property` document: `/users/{userId}/properties/{propertyId}/maintenance_requests/{maintenanceRequestId}`. This ensures that a maintenance request document, for instance, inherently contains the `userId` in its path and also explicitly includes `ownerId` and `propertyId` fields. This eliminates any need for `get()` calls to parent documents (e.g., to check `propertyId` or `ownerId` from the parent property document) within security rules, allowing for atomic operations and easier debugging.

QAPs (Query as Authorization Predicates) are inherently supported by this structure. Because all data related to a user is contained within their `/users/{userId}` subcollections, any `list` or `query` operation performed by an authenticated user will automatically be scoped to only their data. For example, querying `/users/{userId}/properties` will only ever return properties belonging to `request.auth.uid`. Similarly, querying `/users/{userId}/properties/{propertyId}/maintenance_requests` will only return maintenance requests for a property owned by the authenticated user. This structural segregation by `userId` and then `propertyId` ensures that security rules do not need to filter results, but rather `allow` access only to data that meets the implicit authorization predicates of the path and the explicit denormalized fields. This radically simplifies security rules and makes them robust and efficient.
